title: Classic Kachumber Salad
description: Onion, tomato, cucumber salad with lemon and coriander—the everyday Indian side for lunch, dinner, or a light evening snack.
explanation: Kachumber adds raw vegetable volume, crunch, and brightness next to dal-rice or sabzi-roti plates. It is a side, not a full main.
Standard version includes onion, so it is not Jain or no-onion/no-garlic. Soy-free, nut-free, and gluten-free as written.
instructions: Dice cucumber, tomato, and onion to a similar small size. Scoop out watery tomato seeds if the salad should stay crisp longer.
Combine vegetables in a bowl with optional green chilli.
Add lemon juice, roasted cumin, optional chaat masala, and salt.
Serve immediately alongside a main, or as a light evening snack.
stepNotes: Salt draws water—dress just before serving when possible.
For less sharpness, rinse chopped onion under cold water and drain.
English or Japanese cucumbers need no peeling; field cucumbers may.
Chaat masala is optional; lemon and cumin alone are enough.
This is meant to stack with a main plate, not replace it.

variationSections.title: Vegetable add-ins
items: Grated carrot, radish, or capsicum for more color.
Boiled corn kernels for a heartier side.

variationSections.title: Onion-free note
items: For Jain or no-onion versions, skip onion and use extra cucumber, tomato, carrot, and coriander (variation only—not this default recipe).
A pinch of hing in the lemon dressing can add savoriness without onion.
